tasks.title: Temperature Conversion with Range Validation
tasks.description:
Temperature conversion is a fundamental operation in many applications, from weather forecasting to industrial process control. When converting between temperature scales, it is important not only to perform the mathematical transformation but also to validate that the input falls within physically reasonable or application-specific bounds.

Your program must read a temperature value in Celsius and convert it to Fahrenheit. However, you must first check whether the input temperature is within the valid range of −50 to 50 degrees Celsius (inclusive). If the temperature is outside this range, print an error message. If it is valid, compute the Fahrenheit equivalent using the formula F = (C × 9/5) + 32 and print the result rounded to one decimal place.

WORKED EXAMPLE:
Input: 0 → valid, F = 32.0. Input: −40 → valid, F = −40.0. Input: 60 → outside range, error.
